user_query:
I'm preparing a small, local takedown kit for platforms that are hosting unauthorized uploads of my tracks. Please do three coordinated tasks using the files in input/ and save all results under output/:

1) Edit the draft letter at input/cease_and_desist_draft.md into a clean, professional takedown notice and save it to output/cease_and_desist_final.md. Requirements:
- Resolve all placeholders and fix any incorrect details using input/rights_profile.json for my identity/contact and input/works.csv (source of truth) for the exact work titles and ISRCs.
- Use a firm but professional tone. Target length: 300–450 words.
- Include a clear request to remove unauthorized distributions within the response deadline from input/rights_profile.json (response_deadline_hours), and provide the contact email from that same file for written confirmation.
- Include a "Works at issue" section that lists every work from input/works.csv by exact title and ISRC (do not alter punctuation, capitalization, or ISRC formatting). No extra works.

2) Rewrite the outreach emails in input/outreach_emails.jsonl (JSONL with fields: platform, rough_subject, rough_body) into polished messages for each platform and save them to output/outreach_emails_polished.jsonl. For each record:
- Produce a professional, firm but courteous email body of 150–200 words.
- Include a clear subject line.
- Identify the rights holder and include the same contact email as in input/rights_profile.json.
- State the same removal deadline (in hours) as input/rights_profile.json.
- Include a structured "works" array listing every work from input/works.csv with exact title and ISRC. The output JSONL must contain, per line, an object with: platform (string), subject (string), body (string), works (array of objects with keys: title, isrc). Do not add or omit works; titles and ISRCs must match input/works.csv exactly.

3) Modify the configuration at input/notice_config.yaml and write the corrected version to output/notice_config.yaml. Update the following so they directly match input/rights_profile.json and input/works.csv:
- rights_holder, contact.name, contact.email, contact.address, contact.phone, and deadline_hours (set to rights_profile.json.response_deadline_hours).
- Replace the works list so each entry's title and isrc exactly match input/works.csv. Do not change any other keys if present. Ensure the resulting YAML is valid.

Consistency requirements across all outputs:
- The set of works (titles and ISRCs) must be identical in output/notice_config.yaml and in the "works" arrays of every line in output/outreach_emails_polished.jsonl, and must be a verbatim copy of input/works.csv (order is not important).
- The rights_holder and contact email must be the same in the final letter, all polished emails, and output/notice_config.yaml.

Input paths: input/cease_and_desist_draft.md, input/outreach_emails.jsonl, input/notice_config.yaml, input/works.csv, input/rights_profile.json.
Output paths: output/cease_and_desist_final.md, output/outreach_emails_polished.jsonl, output/notice_config.yaml. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
